items.title: Warren Buffett Has Been Dumping Apple Stock for the Past Year. This Is How Much of His Portfolio It Makes Up Right Now.
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: Warren Buffett has been reducing his stake in Apple over the past year, with Apple now making up less than a quarter of Berkshire Hathaway's portfolio. This diversification could be beneficial as the market faces potential vulnerability to a correction.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Buffett remains bullish on Apple's future, but has been selling a significant portion of his stake to secure profits and diversify Berkshire's portfolio.
Buffett's move to diversify Berkshire's portfolio by reducing its heavy reliance on Apple could be beneficial in the face of a potential market correction.

American Express has become one of Berkshire's top holdings, indicating Buffett's confidence in the company.
Bank of America remains one of Berkshire's top holdings, suggesting Buffett's continued faith in the company.
